name: Babaganoush (My Doctored Version)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon ground cumin plus 1 pinch whole
Juice of 1/2 to 1 lemon (to taste, or lime)
Parsley sprigs and a lemon slice for decoration
recipeInstructions:
Sesame paste. Substituting peanut butter is an option, though not tried.
The amounts of ingredients can vary based on personal taste and eggplant size. Try it without celery and red pepper first to decide later usage.
1. Heat olive oil in a wok over medium heat. Add onions (and celery) and cook until they start to brown.
2. Add eggplant and garlic, stirring well.
3. Stir in spices except salt; continue stirring.
4. Reduce heat to low and add salt with tahini and lemon juice. Process or mash briefly to combine; the mixture can be slightly lumpy but too smooth is undesirable.
5. Stir in green herbs and pulse once or twice.
6. Add black olives, parsley sprigs, and a lemon slice for garnish.
7. Serve with pita bread quarters or mini pitas as finger food, perfect with curry meals.
